feedback.text: Hello, similar to dark patterns mentioned in Digital Fairness Act digital software publishers are using EULA to push for full control over sold product, renaming it as service and explicitely stating they are in power to turn off their product or limit user access to it for any reason, not mentioning option to unilateraly change EULA. In these cases consumer is left with no protection. Sold software can be accessed for years or for days, depending on the good will of publishers. Please consider adding this issue to Digital Fairness Act scope. Best Regards
